metadata.title: Guideline on the Core SPC for Human Plasma Derived von Willebrand Factor

rules.rule_text: VWF potency must be measured according to ristocetin cofactor activity (VWF:RCo) compared to the International Standard for VWF concentrate (WHO). FVIII potency must be determined using the Ph. Eur. chromogenic assay.
rules.verbatim_quote: The VWF potency (IU) is measured according to ristocetin cofactor activity (VWF:RCo) <and {specify other methods e.g. collagen binding activity}> compared to the International Standard for von Willebrand factor concentrate (WHO). ... The FVIII potency (IU) is determined using the European Pharmacopoeia chromogenic assay.

rules.rule_text: VWF products are indicated for prevention and treatment of haemorrhage or surgical bleeding in VWD when desmopressin (DDAVP) treatment alone is ineffective or contra-indicated.
rules.verbatim_quote: Prevention and treatment of haemorrhage or surgical bleeding in von Willebrand disease (VWD), when desmopressin (DDAVP) treatment alone is ineffective or contra-indicated.

rules.rule_text: Target levels for VWF treatment: VWF:RCo >0.6 IU/ml (60%) and FVIII:C >0.4 IU/ml (40%) must be achieved. Usual doses are 40-80 IU/kg VWF:RCo and 20-40 IU/kg FVIII:C for FVIII-containing products.
rules.verbatim_quote: Levels of VWF:RCo of > 0.6 IU/ml (60%) and of FVIII:C of > 0.4 IU/ml (40%) should be achieved. ... Usually 40-80 IU/kg of von Willebrand factor (VWF:RCo) and 20-40 IU/kg of FVIII:C are recommended to achieve haemostasis.
rules.exceptions: An initial dose of 80 IU/kg may be required for type 3 VWD.

rules.rule_text: When using FVIII-containing VWF products, continued treatment may cause excessive rise in FVIII:C. After 24-48 hours of treatment, reduced doses and/or prolongation of dose interval or use of a VWF product with low FVIII should be considered.
rules.verbatim_quote: When using a FVIII-containing von Willebrand factor product, the treating physician should be aware that continued treatment may cause an excessive rise in FVIII:C. After 24-48 hours of treatment, in order to avoid an excessive rise in FVIII:C, reduced doses and/or prolongation of the dose interval or the use of a VWF product containing a low level of FVIII should be considered.

rules.rule_text: For VWF products with low FVIII content: haemostasis cannot be ensured until FVIII:C reaches 0.4 IU/ml (40%), which takes 6-12 hours after VWF injection alone. In emergency situations requiring rapid correction, co-administration of a separate FVIII product is necessary.
rules.verbatim_quote: Haemostasis cannot be ensured until factor VIII coagulant activity (FVIII:C) has reached 0.4 IU/ml (40 %). Injection of von Willebrand factor alone does not induce a maximum rise of FVIII:C for at least 6-12 hours. ... if the patient's baseline plasma FVIII:C level is below this critical level, in all situations where a rapid correction of haemostasis should be achieved, such as treatment of haemorrhage, severe trauma or emergency surgery, it is necessary to administer a factor VIII product with the first injection of von Willebrand factor
rules.exceptions: Not needed if baseline FVIII:C is already sufficient for haemostasis.

rules.rule_text: For elective surgery, treatment with VWF should start 12-24 hours before surgery and be repeated 1 hour before the procedure. Co-administration of FVIII is not required if endogenous FVIII:C has reached the critical 0.4 IU/ml level.
rules.verbatim_quote: For elective surgery, treatment with {(Invented) name of product} should start 12-24 hours before surgery and should be repeated 1 hour before the procedure. In this case, co-administration of factor VIII product is not required, since endogenous FVIII:C has usually reached the critical level of 0.4 IU/ml (40 %) before surgery.
rules.exceptions: FVIII:C level should be confirmed in each patient.

rules.rule_text: In actively bleeding patients, it is recommended to co-administer a FVIII product with VWF products having low FVIII content as first-line treatment.
rules.verbatim_quote: In actively bleeding patients it is recommended to co-administer a FVIII product with the von Willebrand factor product with a low FVIII content as a first line treatment.

rules.rule_text: Patients at risk of thrombosis must be monitored for early signs. Prophylaxis against venous thromboembolism must be instituted. In patients receiving FVIII-containing VWF products, FVIII:C levels must be monitored to avoid sustained excessive levels which may increase thrombotic risk.
rules.verbatim_quote: There is a risk of occurrence of thrombotic events, particularly in patients with known clinical or laboratory risk factors. Therefore, patients at risk must be monitored for early signs of thrombosis. Prophylaxis against venous thromboembolism should be instituted, according to the current recommendations. ... In patients receiving factor VIII-containing von Willebrand factor products, plasma levels of FVIII:C should be monitored to avoid sustained excessive FVIII:C plasma levels, which may increase the risk of thrombotic events.

rules.rule_text: Type 3 VWD patients especially may develop neutralising antibodies (inhibitors) to VWF. Inhibitors may occur in close association with anaphylactic reactions. Patients experiencing anaphylaxis must be evaluated for inhibitor presence.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients with von Willebrand disease, especially type 3 patients, may very rarely develop neutralising antibodies (inhibitors) to von Willebrand factor. If such inhibitors occur, the condition will manifest itself as an inadequate clinical response. Such antibodies may occur in close association with anaphylactic reactions. Therefore, patients experiencing anaphylactic reaction should be evaluated for the presence of an inhibitor.

rules.rule_text: Thromboembolic events may occur in case of major VWF overdose.
rules.verbatim_quote: No symptoms of overdose with von Willebrand factor have been reported. Thromboembolic events may occur in case of major overdose.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC PK section must include data on incremental change in VWF and FVIII, AUC for both VWF and FVIII, half-life (initial and elimination) for both, and clearance for both.
rules.verbatim_quote: incremental change in von Willebrand factor and factor VIII ... area under the curve for von Willebrand factor and factor VIII ... half-life (both the initial phase and elimination half life) for von Willebrand factor and factor VIII ... clearance for von Willebrand factor and factor VIII

rules.rule_text: VWF products must be administered only to pregnant and lactating VWF-deficient women if clearly indicated.
rules.verbatim_quote: {(Invented) name of product} should be administered to pregnant and lactating von Willebrand factor deficient women only if clearly indicated.

rules.rule_text: Treatment of VWD must be supervised by a physician experienced in the treatment of haemostatic disorders.
rules.verbatim_quote: Treatment of von Willebrand disease should be supervised by a physician experienced in the treatment of haemostatic disorders.

rules.rule_text: The product must not be mixed with other medicinal products. Only provided injection/infusion sets should be used due to risk of VWF adsorption to equipment surfaces.
rules.verbatim_quote: This medicinal product must not be mixed with other medicinal products. ... Only the provided <injection> <infusion> sets should be used because treatment failure can occur as a consequence of human von Willebrand factor adsorption to the internal surfaces of some <injection> <infusion> equipment.
